lede: A look at January 15 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Molière and Pierre-Joseph Proudhon, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: US Airways Flight 1549
events.desc: US Airways Flight 1549 ditches safely in the Hudson River after the plane collides with birds less than two minutes after take-off. This becomes known as "The Miracle on the Hudson" as all 155 people on board were rescued.

events.title: Wikipedia
events.desc: Wikipedia, a free wiki content encyclopedia, is launched (Wikipedia Day).

events.title: Derveni papyrus
events.desc: The Derveni papyrus, Europe's oldest surviving manuscript dating to 340 BC, is found in northern Greece.

events.title: Black Dahlia
events.desc: The Black Dahlia murder: The dismembered corpse of Elizabeth Short is found in Los Angeles.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The Soviet counter-offensive at Voronezh begins.

events.title: Spanish Civil War
events.desc: Spanish Civil War: Nationalists and Republicans both withdraw after suffering heavy losses, ending the Second Battle of the Corunna Road.

events.title: 1934 Nepal–India earthquake
events.desc: The 8.0 Mw Nepal–Bihar earthquake strikes Nepal and Bihar with a maximum Mercalli intensity of XI (Extreme), killing an estimated 6,000–10,700 people.

events.title: Buffalo Bill Dam
events.desc: Construction ends on the Buffalo Bill Dam in Wyoming, United States, which was the highest dam in the world at the time, at 99 m (325 ft).

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: Fort Fisher in North Carolina falls to the Union, thus cutting off the last major seaport of the Confederacy.

events.title: War of 1812
events.desc: War of 1812: American frigate USS President, commanded by Commodore Stephen Decatur, is captured by a squadron of four British frigates.

births.name: Martin Luther King Jr.
births.line: American minister and activist, Nobel Prize laureate

deaths.text: David Lynch, American television and film director, visual artist and musician, complications from emphysema
Rosa Luxemburg, German economist, theorist, and philosopher

observances.text: Christian feast day: Abeluzius (Ethiopian Orthodox Tewahedo Church)
Christian feast day: Francis Ferdinand de Capillas (one of Martyr Saints of China)
Christian feast day: Our Lady of the Poor
